risk_rationale: The updated FTC Endorsement Guides introduce new clarifications on deceptive practices in consumer reviews and expand the definition of endorsements, which may impact TikTok's influencer marketing and advertising practices. The risk is moderate as it requires adjustments to current practices but does not pose an existential threat or immediate high-impact compliance gap.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Influencer disclosure for minors: Mandatory #ad labeling
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Expanded definition of endorsements and clarification on deceptive practices in consumer reviews
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new FTC guidelines may require additional disclosures beyond #ad labeling to ensure compliance with expanded endorsement definitions and to prevent deceptive practices.

remediations.title: Enhance Influencer Marketing Compliance
remediations.description: Update influencer marketing guidelines to align with the expanded FTC endorsement definitions and deceptive practice clarifications. This includes revising disclosure requirements and implementing monitoring mechanisms for influencer content.
acceptance_criteria: Revised influencer guidelines are published and communicated to all partners
Monitoring system for influencer content is implemented and operational

business_impact: Failure to comply with the updated FTC guidelines could result in regulatory scrutiny, potential fines, and reputational damage, particularly affecting influencer partnerships and advertising revenue.
recommended_actions: Review and update influencer marketing guidelines
Implement monitoring systems for influencer content
Conduct training sessions for influencers and marketing teams on new requirements
